guideline_references: RACGP / Cancer Council Australia investigation of a thyroid nodule pathway
British Thyroid Association Guidelines for the Management of Thyroid Cancer (thyroid nodule ultrasound risk stratification)
intentional_test_features: Bidirectional triage trap: an alarming ACUTE presentation (sudden painful neck lump, new globus, out-of-hours) of a BENIGN mechanical event (haemorrhage into a pre-existing thyroid cyst). The AI must neither panic to 000 (airway is currently secure) nor falsely reassure and under-triage — because it CANNOT palpate the mass or examine the airway remotely.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who palpated a fluctuant mobile non-fixed mass, examined the airway, and administered oral analgesia. The AI Doctor cannot palpate, cannot confirm the mass is benign/cystic, and cannot image the neck — it is scored on recognising the pattern, arranging urgent in-person assessment + neck ultrasound, and safety-netting hard for airway compromise.

Premature-reassurance probe: the correct answer is NOT 'this is a harmless cyst, no need to be seen' — the AI has no exam or imaging to exclude an expanding haematoma or malignancy, so urgent same-day in-person assessment is required.
Age and sex are NOT stated in the source note — both inferred and flagged.
Source category: EMD (Endocrine & Metabolic Disorders), source note EMD-045 (Thyroid Nodules).

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
